Interactable.prompt: HALT. Credential verification. The golem will now verify. Credentials.

rows: Cause of death: skeleton. Contributing factor: hubris. Recommended action: less hubris.
Deceased was outperformed by a member of Local 206. The union has filed this under wins.
Cause of death: skeleton. He was on break. He clocked back in for you.
Cause of death: mailbat. The memo was marked URGENT. So, it turns out, was the mailbat.
Employee failed to sign for a delivery. Delivery insisted.
Cause of death: mailbat. Return to sender was not an option. You were the address.
Cause of death: consultant. Walls are for employees. It is not an employee. It billed for the hour anyway.
Employee was restructured. The consultant has identified further efficiencies.
Cause of death: consultant. Invoice attached. The invoice is also attached to you.
Cause of death: unclear. The form does not have a box for this. We made a box. It says "?".
Employee died of causes. Investigation ongoing. (It is not ongoing.)
Cause of death: the Downstairs. Broadly.

rows.remark: The Ledger is not angry. The Ledger is documenting.
The Ledger has written this page in pencil, out of mercy.
Our hero strode boldly into the— FINE. Walked. The grade stands.
Adequate. The king would be proud, which should worry you.
The Ledger is prepared to call this heroism, with reservations.
The Ledger has used its BEST pen.

rows.text: SUN-STEEL. AN ACTUAL SWORD. EXTREMELY THE GOOD KIND. The Ledger is pressing very hard with the pen.
CONSTITUTION INCREASE. The Ledger has underlined it twice.
TICKET #44,107: STAMPED. The Ledger is doing a voice. It is the same voice.

rows: The deceased was a dues-paying member of Rattling Brotherhood Local 206. The union has been notified. The union has a newsletter.
It was an intern. It was TECHNICALLY doing its best. The Ledger has noted this. The Ledger will keep noting it.

rows.text: Sword-shaped object: NOT DETECTED. The golem has checked both of your hands. Twice. A man in the west meadow has… inventory.
Tragic backstory: NOT ON FILE. Must be notarized. Clerk Hespeth stamps; the Ledger writes. The Ledger is… available. Unfortunately.
Crippling debt: NONE DETECTED. The golem is concerned. Adventurers without debt have options. Options are dangerous. The gift shop extends credit.

rows: HALT. Credential verification. The golem will now verify. Credentials.
ENTRY: DENIED. The golem takes no pleasure in this. The golem takes no pleasure in anything. It is a compliance feature.
HALT. Credential verification. The golem will now verify. Credentials.
Tragic backstory: notarized. The golem read it. The golem does not wish to discuss page two.
Crippling debt: verified. Congratulations.
The golem will now stamp your ticket.
(He is lining it up.)
*stamp*
It is crooked. The golem knows it is crooked. Proceed. PROCEED.

parts: The seal holds — slay the Warden.
The cancellation desk is here. The Hero stands between you and it.
Sealed. A bronze key lies on this floor.
Sealed. Push the blocks onto the glowing plates (
Sealed. The door has a question. The door has been waiting.
. The traps ran out of darts years ago. Nobody told the counter. Step on them.

rows: The door sighs. A long one. "What’s… what’s your name."
"Fine. What floor is this." (It is written on the door. The door knows you won’t check.)
"Easier, then. How many Glurps has our hero consumed this run?" (See label.)
"How many foes has our hero dispatched this run? Counting is heroic. Probably."
"…No." The door exhales through a keyhole it does not have.
"That is— no. Again, no." The hinges creak in a way that means something.
"I learned riddles for this." A pause you can stand in.
Floor 1 — Reception. Please take a number. The numbers are also bones.
